briefdescription.para: Number of nodes in the graph.
Get truth features for the entire graph.

parameterdescription.para: Model type (used to get device index).
Pointer to model for device information.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the tensor, or nullptr if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get data features for the entire graph.

simplesect.para: Pointer to tensor [2, N_edges] with source/target node indices.
Pointer to weight tensor for loss weighting.
Pointer to tensor [N_nodes] indicating which graph each node belongs to.

briefdescription.para: Get tensor of batched event indices.

simplesect.para: Pointer to tensor with original event indices.

briefdescription.para: Check if a feature exists and get its tensor.

simplesect.para: Pointer to tensor, or nullptr if not found.

briefdescription.para: Transfer all tensors to a specific device.
Purge all tensor data and free memory.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::map< std::string, int > *loc, std::map< int, std::vector< torch::Tensor > > *container, std::string name, int dev_)
memberdef.name: return_any

briefdescription.para: Data structure holding graph tensors for ML training and inference.

detaileddescription.para: The graph_t structure contains all tensor data for a single graph or batch of graphs. It manages feature tensors, edge indices, and device placement for both CPU and CUDA.

sect1.title: Accessing Features
sect1.para:
Use the getter methods to access features by name and device:
get_data_node(): Get node input features
get_truth_node(): Get node target labels
get_data_edge(): Get edge input features
get_truth_edge(): Get edge target labels
get_data_graph(): Get graph-level input features
get_truth_graph(): Get graph-level target labels
get_edge_index(): Get edge connectivity [2, E]
get_batch_index(): Get batch assignment per node

sect1.title: Device Management
sect1.para: Tensors can be transferred to CUDA devices using transfer_to_device(). Each device maintains its own copy of the tensors.
